channel.title: Jackson Chen's Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on software engineering, AI, and building things

item.title: The Demo-to-Production Gap in AI Products
item.description: The gap between 'demo' and 'production' is where most AI startups die. It's not the model — it's the 10,000 edge cases around it.

item.title: WebGPU for Browser-Based ML Inference
item.description: Early experiments running a small transformer entirely client-side — what worked, what didn't, and why this changes everything.

item.title: What TikTok Scale Teaches You About Systems
item.description: When your 'edge case' affects 50 million users, there are no edge cases. Working on distributed systems at this scale changes how you think about everything.

item.title: How Rust's Ownership Model Rewired My Brain
item.description: Even in TypeScript now, I instinctively think about who 'owns' a piece of data. Rust didn't just teach me a language — it changed how I reason about state.

item.title: AI Coding: Context Is Everything
item.description: The core of AI-assisted coding isn't letting AI write code — it's letting AI understand your codebase context to make decisions that fit the architecture.

item.title: Open Source Is About Trust, Not Code
item.description: GSoC taught me that open source isn't about code — it's about building trust with a community of strangers through consistent, transparent work.
